phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 90.6 dBA, gradually settling to 71.6-81.6 dBA midway through session. Speech event detected at 216s mark with confidence 0.53. Very loud environment detected; concentration significantly compromised.
phase_1.vitals: Cardiovascular data shows average heart rate of 78 bpm (elevated but acceptable for engaged concentration). Consistent rate suggests maintained focus.
phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (5.1m) that improves to 1.9m as session progresses. Movement sensors indicate rapid transition to stillness; no significant repositioning detected.

phase_2.correlations: Correlation analysis reveals moderate compatibility between positional, kinetic, and acoustic factors.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Analysis indicates excellent concentration potential: quiet audio environment (81.6 dB), settled posture, and favorable physiology.

phase_3.recommendations: Seek quieter environment immediately—current >75dB threshold severely impacts focus. Moderate session length adequate; potential to extend to 366s for skill building.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (81.6dB), user-state stability (settled), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (244s).
